DISCOVERY 301, RULING - THE MATTER WAS CALLED TWICE AND THE COURT HEARD THE ARGUMENTS ON THE PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION IN THE LAW & MOTION CALENDAR BEFORE MAKING A RULING ON THIS MATTER. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS DENNIS MCCARTHY, WILLIAM DOBBIN AND REVA BAILEY'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANTS TO RESPOND TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, DOCUMENT DEMAND AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AND IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS DAVID CHOO AND CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE REALTY INCORPORATED'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION ARBITRATION IS COMPELLED AS TO PLAINTIFF MCCARTHY'S CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANTS CHOO AND C M R EXCEPT FOR THE REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF UNDER BUSINESS AND PROFESSION CODE SECTION 17200. ARBITRATION IS COMPELLED AS TO PLAINTIFFS BAILEY AND DONNINS CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANTS CHOO AND C M R TO THE EXTENT THEY ARISE OUT OF INVESTMENTS IN FUNDS 1 AND 2 EXCEPT FOR THE CLAIM FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF UNDER BUSINESS AND PROFESSION CODE SECTION 172OO. THE PARTIES SHALL ARBITRATE BEFORE JAMS UNLESS THEY AGREE TO A DIFFERENT ARBITRATOR. THE ENTIRE CASE IS STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 301/EHG

Defendants David Choo And California Realty'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
Off Calendar Nov-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JONATHAN BORNSTEIN OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BORNSTEIN & BORNSTEIN, TEL. NO. 409-7611 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. ALEX TALARIDES OF THE ORRICK HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE, LLP, TEL. NO. 773-4254 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: Defendants David Choo And California Mortgage Realty Incorporated's Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action ARBITRATION IS COMPELLED AS TO PLAINTIFF MCCARTHY'S CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANTS CHOO AND C M R EXCEPT FOR THE REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF UNDER BUSINESS AND PROFESSION CODE SECTION 17200. ARBITRATION IS COMPELLED AS TO PLAINTIFFS BAILEY AND DONNINS CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANTS CHOO AND C M R TO THE EXTENT THEY ARISE OUT OF INVESTMENTS IN FUNDS 1 AND 2 EXCEPT FOR THE CLAIM FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF UNDER BUSINESS AND PROFESSION CODE SECTION 172OO. THE PARTIES SHALL ARBITRATE BEFORE JAMS UNLESS THEY AGREE TO A DIFFERENT ARBITRATOR. THE ENTIRE CASE IS STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =301/EHG
